1.question: What sparked the unusual excitement about religion where young Joseph lived (v. 5)?
options: A revival among the sects, starting with the Methodists
The building of a new temple

1.question: Amid the war of words, what question did Joseph keep asking himself (v. 10)?
options: Who of all these parties is right, or are they all wrong?

1.title: A verse that changed history
1.text: In verse 11 Joseph reads James 1:5 — 'If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God' — and says no passage ever came with more power to his heart. That single verse sent him to the grove to pray.

1.question: As Joseph began to pray, what happened before he was delivered (vv. 14-15)?
options: A pillar of light descended over his head
Thick darkness seized him and bound his tongue
An angel took him by the hand

1.title: The First Vision
1.text: Verses 16-17 record the heart of the account: two Personages appeared, and one, pointing to the other, said, 'This is My Beloved Son. Hear Him!' Joseph then asked which church he should join.

1.question: How did the Methodist preacher react when Joseph told him of the vision (v. 21)?
options: He treated it with great contempt, saying it was all of the devil

1.question: By the end of the First Vision episode, what had Joseph found to be true (v. 26)?
options: That he should join the Baptists
The testimony of James — that one who lacks wisdom may ask of God

1.question: How did Joseph describe the messenger who appeared at his bedside (vv. 30-31)?
options: In a loose robe of exquisite whiteness, his person glorious, his countenance like lightning

1.title: Moroni's night visits
1.text: On the night of September 21, 1823, an angel appeared three times, each time repeating the same message. He named himself Moroni and said God had a work for Joseph to do (v. 33).

1.question: What did the messenger say was written upon gold plates (v. 34)?
options: An account of the former inhabitants of this continent and the fulness of the Gospel

1.question: Quoting the prophecy of Elijah, the messenger said the hearts of the children would turn to whom (v. 39)?
After the messenger finished speaking, how did he leave the room (v. 43)?
options: He ascended through a conduit opened into heaven

1.question: On the third visit, what did the messenger warn Satan would tempt Joseph to do (v. 46)?
options: To get the plates in order to get rich

1.question: When Joseph told his father about the vision, what did his father reply (v. 50)?
options: That it was of God, and to do as commanded
That he should keep it secret forever
That he must consult a priest

1.template: there indeed did I behold the plates, the {0}, and the breastplate

1.question: The messenger forbade Joseph from taking the plates then; how often was he to return to the place (v. 54)?
options: Once, precisely one year later, and each year after

1.question: Where did Joseph find the plates deposited (vv. 51-52)?
options: Under a stone of considerable size, in a stone box on a hill near Manchester

1.question: What common story arose from Joseph working for Josiah Stoal (v. 56)?
options: That he had been a money-digger
That he owned a silver mine

1.question: On what date did the messenger finally deliver the plates to Joseph (v. 59)?
Who tore up the certificate, saying there was no such thing as the ministering of angels (v. 65)?

1.template: Two days after the arrival of Mr. Cowdery (being the 7th of April) I commenced to translate the {0}

1.title: Restoration of the Priesthood
1.text: In May 1829, while translating, Joseph and Oliver Cowdery prayed about baptism. A heavenly messenger — John the Baptist — laid hands on them and conferred the Aaronic Priesthood (vv. 68-69).
